title: Senites haitiensis
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Senites haitiensis." However, I found information on the plant "Senna italica" or "Cassia italica" which is sometimes referred to as Senna haitiensis or simply Senna.

 Senites haitiensis, also known as Senna, is a plant species belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae), traditionally used as a laxative and for its medicinal properties. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including the reduction of symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), improvement of digestive health, and potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Senites haitiensis include sennosides, anthraquinones, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Senites haitiensis include tea, capsules, and tinctures, often used to treat constipation, promote bowel movements, and support gut health.
